what_it_does: This file is a draft design document (read-only investigation, not built) that lays out how to wire the existing file-discovered forms registry into the capture/ingest pipeline, including effort routing, scoping, form authoring, and per-shape input/output sizing.
observations: Status is 'draft (design-grounding — read-only investigation, NOT built)' and dated 2026-06-17.
The existing forms registry in runtime/forms.py + forms/*.py is complete and tested but wired to nothing: no capture path calls route().
run_items fans one role over N units at one max_tokens and calls run_role without policy=, so a form's policy cannot currently reach the model.
Proposes a bucket-by-band capture wrapper that routes each unit via route(text, meta), groups by {stage, policy}, drops 'skip' units with named logging, and fires run_items once per band.
Proposes adding a policy= passthrough from run_items to run_role, with by_form=False default to preserve today's exact behavior.
Proposes a scope dimension (global/project/user) mirroring roles.mode_scope, defaulting to global so current forms stay unchanged.
route() would filter by in-scope forms and pick via a 2-axis sort (scope_rank, fallthrough_rank, id) to prevent a user fallthrough from masking a global narrow form.
There is no active-project singleton; project scope is fed from the explicit project argument already present on capture calls.
Highlights that create_form 'on the existing gate' is impossible while match is a Python callable, because the shared _write_registry_file gate refuses callable fields.
Presents three options for the match/create_form fork (A: keep callable and build a new code-render gate; B: convert match to RULE_OPS data-AST and reuse the shared gate; C: hybrid, recommended).
Recommends Option C: data-AST forms are create_form-able via the shared gate; callable forms remain developer-gated.
Proposes two new optional form fields, context and lens, plus a stage-to-band sizing registry to replace the WALK_MAX_CHARS hardcode on the form-aware path.
The four current forms are treated as placeholders; the document designs mechanism, not taxonomy.
Cites file:line references and labels claims as Observed or Inferred design.
standouts: The 'hollow wire' diagnosis: route() exists, but without run_items/run_role changes effort routing would only relabel, not retier.
The create_form fork is framed as a schema-contract decision that must go to Tim before any build.
The 2-axis precedence rule (scope_rank × fallthrough_rank × id) for resolving scoped form collisions.
The 'form as dossier' concept: a form sizes both the assembled input (context/slice) and the allowed output (policy/lens).
conventions: Follows the module's 'data over hardcode' and file-discovered registry pattern.
Uses behavior-preserving defaults (by_form=False, scope default global) to avoid changing existing paths.
Mirrors prior art: mode_scope for scoping, RULE_OPS for predicates, ContextRegistry for context packages, GenerationPolicy for output controls.
Labels epistemic status explicitly (Observed vs Inferred design) and provides file:line citations.
Fail-loud and no-silent-drop floor: skip units are named, not silently discarded.
Reuse-don't-parallel: bucket loop calls run_items once per band rather than adding a second fan path.
concerns: The entire design is unbuilt and self-described as read-only; many pieces require a Tim decision before implementation.
Option C hybrid leaves two match representations (callable and data-AST) in one schema, increasing validation and evaluation complexity.
The required RULE_OPS shape-recognition operations (regex-on-head, line-ratio, etc.) are not concretely specified.
Scope generalization to other registries is deferred to a future shared base, risking later duplication if not promoted.
The effort-band registry is proposed but its exact form (separate registry vs inline fields) is left as a decision point.
notes: A draft build plan for making the forms registry actually drive capture effort, scoped resolution, and authoring, with a big red flag that the matcher representation must be decided by Tim first.
questions: What exact shape-recognition operations will be added to RULE_OPS if Option C is chosen?
Which option will Tim choose for the match/create_form fork before implementation begins?
Will the effort-band registry be a separate file-discovered registry or optional fields on each Form row?
How will context:// packages be assembled and prepended to units in the bucket loop?
What is the intended user identifier value today, and how will it thread through capture call lineage?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file designs the wiring, scoping, authoring, and sizing mechanisms it claims to, while also diagnosing blockers and surfacing decisions.
apparent_themes: Data-over-code for authorability; executable code stays gated
Reuse existing plumbing rather than create parallel paths
Fail-loud and explicit (no silent drops, no ambient singletons)
Ground design in observed code facts
intention_signals: To provide a grounded, low-risk implementation plan for connecting forms to capture and to force a Tim decision on the high-stakes matcher-authoring contract before any code is written.
novelty: It reframes a seemingly straightforward 'wire forms' task into a diagnosis that the wire would be hollow without run_items/run_role changes, and reframes create_form as a schema-contract fork rather than a routine gate reuse.
contribution: This file contributes a detailed, citation-grounded design sketch with explicit option analysis and risk flags for integrating forms into capture; it would be kept as the rationale and decision record for the bucket-by-band routing mechanism and the callable-vs-data-AST matcher fork.
summary_for_embedding: Draft design document for wiring the existing file-discovered forms registry into the capture pipeline via bucketed effort bands, scoped resolution, a policy passthrough, and an unresolved create_form authoring fork between callable and data-AST matchers.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to guide implementation of a working forms-driven capture routing system and to force a Tim decision on how form matchers are authored.
